California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 26, 1989 
James W. March, Esq. 
AmBase Corporation of New York 
59 Maiden Lane 
New York, New York 10038 
Dear Mr. March: 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. A-89-520 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you on behalf of AmBase's California and Federal Political 
Action Committees concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of 
the Political Reform Act. 
Your letter, dated August 31, 1989, correctly summarizes the 
advice I provided to you. 
A copy of your letter is enclosed for 
your convenience. 
If you have any additional questions, please contact me at 
(916) 
322-5662. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: 
Alice Hughes 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660

August 31, 1989

AIV8ASE 
In accordance with our recent conversation, enclosed is my 
letter to Cindy Durham of the "Pete Wilson for Governor" campaign 
staff along with a copy of the check for the $2,500 contribution to 
"Pete Wilson for Governor." 
Please note that the check was issued 
by 
ArnBase's 
California 
Registered Political 
Action 
Committee 
(ArnBase 
Political 
Action 
Committee 
of 
California). 
This 
contribution was made after we received reirnbursemant from the 
Wilson campaign of the $2,500 contribution made by our Federal 
Political Action Committee 
(ArnBase Corporation Political Action 
Committee) . 
The reirnbursemant to our Federal PPC and. subsequent $2,500 
contribution by our California PPC corrpletes the unwinding of a 
process that began with the $2,500 contribution made to Pete Wilson 
from our- Federal PPC in May, 1989 . 
Shortly after this contribution 
was made, you informed me that a Federal PPC could contribute only 
$1,000 to a candidate for state office in California. 
As noted in 
my correspondence of July 12, and. in accordance with our earlier 
discussion, 
we atterrpted to correct this error by having our 
California 
PPC 
reimburse 
our 
Federal 
PAC 
for 
the 
$2,500 
contribution it made to Pete Wilson. 
In late July, 
I asked Stephanie Fitzgerald of the Federal 
Elections Commission (FEC) how the $2,500 contribution should be 
reported in our semi -annual FEC filing. 
Ms. Fi tzgerald informed me 
that we should not have reimbursed our Federal PPC since FEC 
regulations prohibit transfers from non-federal accounts. 
Although 
Ms. 
Fitzgerald understood that the transfer was not made to 
circumvent the FEC rule, she strongly advised that we transfer the 
rroney back to the California PPC. 
ve followed Ms. Fitzgerald's 
advice and on August 9, 1989 our Federal PPC issued a $2,500 check 
to our California PPC. 
AmBase CorporallDO of NeW 'fork 


115. Alice Hughes 
August 31, 1989 
Page 2

from the California PAC to the Federal PM::. both took place prior to 
July 
1, 
1989 
and were 
therefore reported in the 
Fonn 
420 
sEmi-annual filing covering the January 1, 1989 -
June 30, 1989 
pericxi. 
The reimburSEment by Wilson of the $2,500 Federal PM::. 
cont.ribution and the $2,500 contribution by the California PM::. to 
Wilson were made after July 1, 1989 and will therefore be reported 
in the next Fonn 420 sEmi-annual statEment, to be sutmitted by 
January 31, 1990. 
Please advise if we should sul::mit any other 
official report of these post July 1, 1989 transactions. 
On behalf of the AmBase Corporation, 
I 
thank you for your 
patience and understanding. 
Your assistance has been invaluable. 
JWM:lr

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 6, 1989 
James W. March 
AmBase Corporation of New York 
59 Maiden Lane 
New York, NY 
10038 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-520 
Dear Mr. March: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on September I, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
(l 
~::::-~;;:chard 
J 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question_synthetic: AmBase Corporation sought confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper reporting and contribution limits under California's Political Reform Act for its federal and state political action committees' contributions to the Pete Wilson for Governor campaign.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the requestor's letter accurately summarized the telephone advice previously provided regarding campaign disclosure provisions and the correction process for the over-limit federal PAC contribution.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter (Aug 31) and FPPC response letter (Sept 26), plus related correspondence. No explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections in formal format; advice was initially provided by telephone and confirmed in writing. Confidence reduced due to informal nature of initial advice and multiple supporting documents rather than single formatted letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: AmBase Corporation sought confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper reporting and contribution limits under California's Political Reform Act for its federal and state political action committees' contributions to the Pete Wilson for Governor campaign.

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated August 31, 1989, correctly summarizes the advice I provided to you.

embedding.summary: This file documents the FPPC's confirmation of telephone advice to AmBase Corporation regarding contribution limits and reporting requirements after the requestor's federal PAC inadvertently contributed $2,500 (exceeding the $1,000 limit for federal PACs to California state candidates) and subsequently corrected the error through reimbursement and a compliant contribution from its California PAC.
